items.text:
The Grind House Café — Study-Hour Specials

Located on the ground floor of Kessler Library, The Grind House Café serves coffee, light meals, and snacks daily from 7:00 AM to 11:00 PM. During finals week, we extend our specials to help you power through late-night study sessions.

Coffee & Tea
- Drip Coffee (12 oz): $2.00
- Café Latte (16 oz): $3.75
- Herbal Tea: $2.25

Food
- Bagel with Cream Cheese: $3.00
- Turkey & Cheddar Panini: $6.50
- Veggie Wrap: $5.75

Study-Hour Special: Every day from 9:00 PM to 11:00 PM, show your student ID and get any drip coffee for $1.00 plus a free refill on the same cup before closing. The discount cannot be combined with other promotions and applies only to drip coffee, not lattes or tea.

We also offer a Semester Punch Card for $10.00: buy nine drinks at regular price and receive the tenth free. Punch cards must be used before the last day of the semester and are non-transferable between students.

Payment is accepted by campus card, credit card, or mobile wallet; we no longer accept cash. Please recycle your cup in the bin near the exit when you're finished. Questions about catering for study groups can be directed to the counter staff or emailed to grindhouse@campus.edu.

questions.stem: According to the menu, what must a student do to receive the Study-Hour Special discount?

options.A: Purchase a Semester Punch Card in advance
options.B: Show a student ID after 9:00 PM
options.C: Order a latte instead of drip coffee
options.D: Pay for the drink using cash at the counter

questions.explanation: The menu states that from 9:00 PM to 11:00 PM, students who show their student ID can get drip coffee for $1.00 with a free refill. Option A confuses the punch card, a separate offer. Option C reverses the rule, since the special applies only to drip coffee, not lattes. Option D contradicts the passage, which says cash is no longer accepted.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this menu?

options.A: To announce the café's move to a new building
options.B: To explain how to apply for a meal plan
options.C: To present café offerings, prices, and discounts
options.D: To recruit student volunteers for the counter

questions.explanation: The passage lists drink and food prices along with the study-hour discount and punch card promotion, so its overall purpose is to present the café's offerings and special pricing. Option A invents a relocation not mentioned. Option B confuses the punch card with a meal plan. Option D introduces a recruitment purpose absent from the text.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Semester Punch Card?

options.A: It expires before a student graduates from the university
options.B: It is meant to reward students who buy drinks regularly
options.C: It replaces the need for a campus card at checkout
options.D: It offers a bigger discount than the Study-Hour Special

questions.explanation: Since the punch card rewards a customer with a free tenth drink after nine paid purchases, it is designed to encourage repeat, regular buying. Option A overstates a graduation-based expiration; the passage only mentions the semester's last day. Option C wrongly implies the card substitutes for payment methods. Option D makes an unsupported comparison of discount size between the two promotions.

items.text:
Subject: Your Tutoring Appointment Confirmed — Thursday, March 12

Dear Ms. Alvarado,

This message confirms your appointment with the Bellwood Tutoring Center for Thursday, March 12, from 3:30 PM to 4:30 PM. You'll be meeting with tutor Mr. Osei in Room 214 of the Academic Success Building to review your Calculus II problem sets.

Please bring your textbook, a copy of the assignment you're struggling with, and a photo ID to check in at the front desk. Sessions that start more than ten minutes late may be shortened to protect the next student's scheduled time, so we recommend arriving a few minutes early. If you can't make it, we'd appreciate at least 24 hours' notice so another student on the waitlist can take the slot.

Each student receives four free sessions per semester; after that, drop-in sessions are $8.00 per hour. Since this is your first visit this semester, today's session will not count against your four free sessions, so don't worry about the limit yet.

If you need to reschedule, just reply to this email or call the front desk at 555-0173. We're open Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM.

Best regards,
The Bellwood Tutoring Center Team

format_metadata.subject: Your Tutoring Appointment Confirmed — Thursday, March 12

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Ms. Alvarado bring to check in at the front desk?

options.A: Her textbook, the assignment, and a photo ID
options.B: Her laptop, a calculator, and a class syllabus
options.C: Her signed waiver and a billing statement
options.D: Her class schedule and a copy of her transcript

questions.explanation: The email directly instructs her to bring her textbook, a copy of the assignment she's struggling with, and a photo ID. Option B substitutes unrelated items never mentioned. Option C invents a waiver and billing statement. Option D fabricates a schedule and transcript requirement.

questions.stem: The word 'shortened' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, a session that is 'shortened' has its length reduced, not eliminated, so D is correct. A ('cancelled') overstates the effect, since the session still happens. B ('postponed') wrongly suggests a time change rather than a duration change. C ('recorded') introduces a meaning unrelated to time.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Ms. Alvarado's use of the tutoring center this semester?

options.A: She will be charged $8.00 for today's session
options.B: She still has all four free sessions available after today
options.C: She has exceeded her limit of free sessions this term
options.D: She must switch to a different tutor after this visit

questions.explanation: Because the email states that today's session, her first this semester, will not count against her four free sessions, she still has all four remaining afterward. Option A reverses the fee rule, which applies only after the free sessions are used. Option C contradicts the passage's explicit statement that she needn't worry about the limit yet. Option D introduces a tutor-switching requirement not mentioned.
